msgs.text:
I read the problem statement and the prior-turn log as external proposals. The prompt’s required submission format is still the four-generator tame format with distinguished sigma,tau, implicit tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2, later generators in the pro-2 core, and constants reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log’s only serious unrefuted candidate remains the “dynamic tame-inertia” word; the earlier scalar, plain-π2, and fixed-τ−2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with π=π2∈Z and η=2π on odd tame inertia,r=xσ−πxσπx−1(xτ−η)π ((yτ)π)σy−1,with π≡40491355905,−π≡45176306175,−η≡50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of −η is immaterial in this occurrence, because that exponent is applied only to the tame inertia generator τ, whose finite tame images have odd order.The stable candidate-side algebra from the late prior turns remains:Da=j=0∑π−1(tαa)j on a nontrivial odd tame-character sign-cover block satisfies (1−tαa)Da=1−(tαa)π=1−t,soDa=(1−t)(1−tαa)−1.

That is the direction that makes Da radical and −1+D−η a unit. Also, in the trivial tame-inertia block, the safe elimination convention is (1+T)Ps (T)=1+T−s,s≡π(modf),up to the harmless T↔T−1 convention. With these conventions, the prior candidate-side abelianization and sign-kernel conclusions remain internally consistent:Ue,fab≃Z2ef+1⊕C2,(kerκ)ab≃Z22ef+1⊕C4.New Turn 77 contribution: the b[2]-coordinate vanishes for every unramified Frobenius U2 -lift

Turns 74–76 reopened the universal square coordinate ϵb. Turn 74 correctly rejected the argument b∨=[−1]; Turn 75 correctly rejected the replacement b∨=[5] by showing that, in the unramified quadratic field L=Q2 (ω), the natural b-class is represented by B=1−4ω, not by 5. Turn 76 then checked directly that

F(B)≡Bv2 (modL×4),so ϵb=0 in that one test.I can now generalize the mechanism. The rational-unit normalization B=5 is unnecessary; the vanishing follows from a general U2 -unit calculation.

Let L/Q2 be unramified, let F be arithmetic Frobenius, and let B∈1+4OL be any U2 -unit representative of the invariant free b-line. Write

B=1+4β,β∈OL,and setA=BF(B).Then

A∈(1+2OL)2⋅L×4.Indeed, put d=F(β)−β. Modulo 2,dˉ=βˉ2+βˉ, sodˉ lies in the Artin–Schreier image a↦a2+a on the residue field. Since X2+X−d has unit derivative, Hensel lifting gives a∈OLwitha+a2=d.Let U=1+2a. ThenU2=1+4(a+a2)=1+4d.NowU2A=1+4(Fβ−β)(1+4Fβ)/(1+4β)∈1+16OL.For an unramified dyadic field, the 2-adic logarithm identifies 1+4OL with 4OL, and fourth powers from 1+4OL have logarithms in 16OL. Hence 1+16OL⊂L×4.

ThereforeBF(B)≡U2 (modL×4),with U∈1+2OL. This correction is a square of a regular principal unit, not a b-square. Thus the b[2]coordinate is zero for every unramified Frobenius action on a U2 -normalized b-representative.

This recovers Turn 76 as the case L=Q2 (ω), B=1−4ω, where one may take U congruent to the regular unit v=1+2(1+ω) modulo squares. It also explains why the false B=5 normalization was not needed: the real invariant is not that B descends from Q2×, but that B lies in 1+4OL, so F(B)/B is a regular-unit square modulo fourth powers.

Consequence The first-IA obstruction should again be viewed as essentially mixed anti-cyclotomic, but for the corrected reason:(ϵb,R(T))⇝R(T),provided the b-line is represented by its natural U2 -unit lift and the comparison uses the regular-square correction above. The S[2]-coordinate remains fixed by the marked uniformizer class. What still has not been computed is the mixed residualRc (T)∈F2[Cc]for primitive anti-cyclotomic orders c=3,5,9,11,…. The prior detector remains the right one:Δc(T)=ℓ∣c∏(1+T c/ℓ),and the primitive c-part vanishes exactly whenΔc(T)Rc (T)=0.

So the next genuine arithmetic work is still to compute Rc (T), starting with c=3, then c=5, c=9, and c=11. The new lemma only removes the universal b[2]coordinate from the obstruction package under the correct U2 -unit normalization; it does not touch the mixed commutator residuals.

ESTABLISHED: The dynamic candidate remains the only unrefuted candidate. I generalized Turn 76’s unramified-quadratic ϵb=0 computation: for every unramified L/Q2 and every U2 -unit b-representative B=1+4β, one has

F(B)/B≡U2 (modL×4)for some regular principal unit U∈1+2OL. Thus the b[2]-coordinate vanishes without any false rational normalization B=5.

GAP: The presentation is still not proved. The mixed anti-cyclotomic arithmetic-minus-candidate residuals Rc (T) have not been computed, and no dyadic formation theorem has been supplied to force their vanishing or to control higher filtered layers.

NEXT: Compute Rc (T) for c=3,5,9,11, using the corrected U2 -normalization for the b-line and the primitive detector Δc(T)Rc (T). A nonzero primitive component refutes the candidate; vanishing moves the proof obligation to the next primitive order or to a global dyadic formation theorem.[BREAKTHROUGH]
